name: Bit

unions.name: Bit
unions.comment:
One of 2 states:

  - 1, on: `I`
  - 0, off: `O`

This right here is really the core of this library.
Representing bits safely is now quite readable!

    ArraySized.l8 O I I I O O I O
    --: ArraySized Bit (In (Up8 minX_) (Up8 maxX_))

for an example of using this to represent an `Id`,
visit the [readme](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/lue-bird/elm-bits/latest/)

values.name: fromN
values.type: N.N (N.In min_ (N.Up maxTo1_ N.To N.N1)) -> Bit.Bit

values.name: opposite
values.comment: Switch `O` â†” `I`. Often called the NOT operator

values.name: toN
values.comment:
Convert `O` to `n0`, `I` to `1`.
`N (In (Up0 minX_) (Up1 maxX_))` means that the result will be between 0 & 1

    toInt =
        Bit.toN >> N.toInt

name: Bits
comment:
[`ArraySized`](https://package.elm-lang.org/packages/lue-bird/elm-typesafe-array/latest/)
of [`Bit`](Bit#Bit)s

Use what's available in `ArraySized` to create and analyze bits!

Use the helpers here in `Bits`
when you know the exact number of bits, like in an id


## `N`

@docs fromN, toN


## `Int`

@docs fromIntSigned, toIntSigned


## alter

@docs padToAtLeast, takeAtMost, unpad
@docs toChunksOf

You can additionally use the operations of `ArraySized`, for example

    -- left shift
    ArraySized.attach Up (ArraySized.repeat O n...)

    -- complement
    ArraySized.map Bit.opposite

    ...

values.name: fromIntSigned
values.comment:
Clamp a signed `Int`, decoding a given bit size `n`
ranging from `-(2 ^ (n - 1))` to `2 ^ (n - 1) - 1`.
For example, bit size 5 ranges from -16 to 15

    import ArraySized
    import Bit exposing (Bit(..))
    import N exposing (n5, n6)

    -6 |> Bits.fromIntSigned n5 |> ArraySized.toList
    --> [ O, I, O, I, O ]

    10 |> Bits.fromIntSigned n6 |> ArraySized.toList
    --> [ I, O, I, O, I, O ]
values.type: N.N (N.Exactly (N.On bitSize)) -> Basics.Int -> ArraySized.ArraySized Bit.Bit (N.Exactly (N.On bitSize))

values.name: fromN
values.comment:
Convert the unsigned integer to 32 bits

Combine with [`takeAtMost`](#takeAtMost) to clamp to a lower bit size

    import N exposing (n4, n14)
    import Bit exposing (Bit(..))
    import ArraySized

    n14 |> Bits.fromN |> Bits.takeAtMost n4 |> ArraySized.toList
    --> [ I, I, I, O ]

The `N` is always clamped to `<=  2 ^ 32 - 1`

    import N exposing (n0)

    2 ^ 53 - 1
        |> N.intToAtLeast n0
        |> Bits.fromN
        |> Bits.toN
        |> N.toInt
    --> 2 ^ 32 - 1
values.type: N.N range_ -> ArraySized.ArraySized Bit.Bit (N.In (N.Local.Up32 minX_) (N.Local.Up32 maxX_))

values.name: padToAtLeast
values.comment:
Increase the capacity to a given length
by padding with [`O`](Bit#Bit)s to the beginning

Short for

    ArraySized.padToAtLeast Down lengthMinimum (ArraySized.repeat O)
values.type: N.N (N.In (N.On paddedMin) (N.Up maxX N.To paddedMaxPlusX)) -> ArraySized.ArraySized Bit.Bit (N.In (N.Up paddingMaxPlusX_ N.To paddedMaxPlusX) (N.Up paddingMin_ N.To paddedMin)) -> ArraySized.ArraySized Bit.Bit (N.In (N.On paddedMin) (N.Up maxX N.To paddedMaxPlusX))

values.name: takeAtMost
values.comment:
If the `ArraySized` has a [`I`](Bit#Bit) earlier than a given number of [`Bit`](Bit#Bit)s up,
returns only [`I`](Bit#Bit)s up to the given length

    import N exposing (n0, n16)
    import ArraySized

    2 ^ 25
        |> N.intToAtLeast n0
        |> Bits.fromN
        |> Bits.takeAtMost n16
        |> ArraySized.maxTo n16
        |> Bits.toN
        |> N.toInt
    --> 2 ^ 16 - 1

Its length after that will be `Exactly` the given desired length.
Some operations like attaching or `Bits.toN` will stop working because of this.
In that case, just

    |> Bits.takeAtMost n
    |> ArraySized.minTo n
    |> ArraySized.maxTo n

To give the `ArraySized`'s _type_ more information
values.type: N.N (N.Exactly (N.On newLength)) -> ArraySized.ArraySized Bit.Bit (N.In (N.On min_) max_) -> ArraySized.ArraySized Bit.Bit (N.Exactly (N.On newLength))

values.name: toChunksOf
values.comment:
Pad the `ArraySized` of [`Bit`](Bit#Bit)s to chunks of a given length

    import ArraySized
    import Linear exposing (Direction(..))
    import N exposing (n8)
    import Bit exposing (Bit(..))

    ArraySized.one O
        |> Bits.toChunksOf n8
        |> ArraySized.map ArraySized.toList
        |> ArraySized.toList
    --> [ [ O, O, O, O, O, O, O, O ] ]

    ArraySized.l3 I I I
        |> ArraySized.attach Up (ArraySized.l8 O I I I O I O O)
        |> ArraySized.attach Up (ArraySized.l8 O I I I O I O O)
        |> ArraySized.attach Up (ArraySized.l8 O I I I O I O O)
        |> Bits.toChunksOf n8
        |> ArraySized.map ArraySized.toList
        |> ArraySized.toList
    --> [ [ O, O, O, O, O, I, I, I ]
    --> , [ O, I, I, I, O, I, O, O ]
    --> , [ O, I, I, I, O, I, O, O ]
    --> , [ O, I, I, I, O, I, O, O ]
    --> ]

[`O`](Bit#Bit)s at the beginning aren't removed from the original `ArraySized`
values.type: N.N (N.Exactly (N.On (N.Add1 chunkLengthMinus1))) -> ArraySized.ArraySized Bit.Bit (N.In (N.On min_) (N.Up maxX N.To maxPlusX)) -> ArraySized.ArraySized (ArraySized.ArraySized Bit.Bit (N.Exactly (N.On (N.Add1 chunkLengthMinus1)))) (N.In (N.Up0 minX_) (N.Up maxX N.To (N.Add1 maxPlusX)))

values.name: toIntSigned
values.comment:
Create signed `Int`
encoded in its **length as the `Int`'s bit size**

Encoding as follows:
given bit size `n`,
encode in the range `-(2 ^ (n - 1))` â†’ `2 ^ (n - 1) - 1`.
For example, bit size 5 ranges from -16 to 15

    import ArraySized
    import Bit exposing (Bit(..))
    import N exposing (n5, n6)

    ArraySized.l5 O I O I O |> Bits.toIntSigned
    -- 5-bit `Int`
    --> -6

    ArraySized.l6 I O I O I O |> Bits.toIntSigned
    -- 6-bit `Int`
    --> 10

[`padToAtLeast`](#padToAtLeast) to convert it to a higher-bit representation

    import ArraySized
    import Bit exposing (Bit(..))
    import N exposing (n8)

    ArraySized.l6 I O I O I O
        |> Bits.padToAtLeast n8
        |> Bits.toIntSigned
    -- 8-bit `Int`
    --> -128 + 42
values.type: ArraySized.ArraySized Bit.Bit (N.In min_ (N.Up maxTo32_ N.To N.Local.N32)) -> Basics.Int

values.name: toN
values.comment:
Convert <= 32 bits into an unsigned integer: `N (Min (Up0 nX_))`

    import N
    import Bit exposing (Bit(..))
    import ArraySized

    ArraySized.l6 I O O I O I
        |> Bits.toN
        |> N.toInt
    --> 37
values.type: ArraySized.ArraySized Bit.Bit (N.In min_ (N.Up maxTo32_ N.To N.Local.N32)) -> N.N (N.Min (N.Up0 nX_))

values.name: unpad
values.type: ArraySized.ArraySized Bit.Bit (N.In (N.On min_) max) -> ArraySized.ArraySized Bit.Bit (N.In (N.Up0 minX_) max)
